Energy Minister Affirms Commitment to Vision 2030
General David Petraeus Applauds Reforms of Saudi Vision 2030
Saudi Shipping Industry Embraces Vision 2030Saudi and U.S.
SAUDI VISION 2030
STATUS REPORTENERGY MINISTER AFFIRMS COMMITMENT TO VISION 2030 
?Ć£It is not unthinkable?ĆØ that the price of oil could jump to $60 per barrel by the end of the year, said Saudi Minister of Energy, Industry, and Min-eral Resources Khalid Al-Falih in recent remarks at the 23rd World Energy Congress in Istanbul.
According to Al-Falih, the fluctuating price of oil has not affected the Kingdom?ĆÖs preparations to implement the Vision 2030 plan, which will diversify the Saudi economy and invite investment across a broad array of sectors.
Al-Falih, who also serves as the Chairman of Ar-amco, offered a positive outlook on the future of market growth, noting that the sale of shares in Aramco through its planned IPO will reinvigorate the oil industry.
Under Vision 2030, Aramco will be transformed into a global industrial conglom-erate that can weather international economic insecurity.
Saudi Arabia is among the 20 largest economies in the world, but the nation aspires to rise to 15th place by 2030 through structural reforms and strategic investments in both public and private sectors.
GENERAL DAVID PETRAEUS APPLAUDS REFORMS OF SAUDI VISION 2030 
General David Petraeus, former Director of the Central Intelligence Agency and former Com-mander of U.S.Central Command, recently praised Saudi Arabia for its Vision 2030 reform agenda.
Gen.Petraeus made his remarks at the C3 US-Arab Business Summit, an event dedicated to sharing best practices and transferring knowledge between the United States and the Arab region in the sectors of healthcare, business, and corporate social responsibility.
?Ć£By the way, this is not just economic reform.
It goes into areas of social reform and even areas of political reform in certain respects.
Is it easy? Goodness, no.
This is a transformation of a country.
This is true, true, significant change.But it is happening.?ĆØPetraeus continued: ?Ć£They want to open up all kinds of investment opportunities?Ć?they want to open up every sector to outside investment.
You name the sector, and I will give a thumbs up.?ĆØ 
SAUDI SHIPPING INDUSTRY EMBRACES VISION 2030
Chairman of the Saudi Port Authority Dr. Nabil Al-Amoudi, delivered the keynote address on Sept.25 at a shipping-industry conference, at which he confirmed that plans to link King Abdul Aziz Seaport in Dammam and Jubail Industrial Port through a rail line move forward.
Dr. Al-Amoudi said that the Kingdom was cele-brating World Maritime Day within the framework of Vision 2030, emphasizing that the maritime industry continues to be an important component of growth and development in the Kingdom.
He added that the shipping industry in the Kingdom was ready to accept the challenges laid out in the goals of Vision 2030 and will emerge as a main player in fulfilling the aspirations of the Saudi government in this respect.
To take full advantage of these invest-ments, the government plans to work with the private sector and enter into a new series of international partnerships to improve and complete its infrastruc-ture and link it internally and across borders.
SAUDI AND U.S.RESEARCHERS INNOVATE SOLAR BATTERY
A team of Saudi and U.S.researchers at King Abdullah University of Science and Technology and the University of Wisconsin-Madison have created a new proof-of-concept device that could be a major breakthrough in the efficiency of solar energy.
The researchers have combined a solar cell with  a large-capacity liquid battery, bypassing the electricity-making process to transfer the harvest-ed solar energy directly into the battery.
Under Vision 2030, Saudi Arabia has set itself an initial target of generating 9.5 gigawatts of renew-able energy.
The government will also seek to localize a significant portion of the renewable-energy val-ue chain in the Saudi economy, including research and development and manufacturing, among other stages.
The researchers at King Abdullah University of Science in Technology, along with their U.S.counter-parts, fit into a new push by the Kingdom to increase educational opportunities that fit with the demands of the job market.
BOEING OPENS RESEARCH & DEVELOPMENT OFFICE AT KAUST
King Abdullah University of Science and Technology recently announced the opening of a Boeing Research and Technology (BR&T) office within the University?ĆÖs Research and Technology Park.
As the first of its kind in Saudi Arabia and the Gulf region, the office will foster an institutional partnership in aerospace innovation.
Formal knowledge-exchange agreements between KAUST and Boeing have been in place since 2009 under the KAUST Industry Collaboration Program (KICP).
Ahmed Jazzar, President of Boeing in Saudi Arabia, said: ?Ć£KAUST is a strategic partner to Boeing, and we as a company are committed to Saudi Arabia?ĆÖs R&D community.?ĆØ Also placing the emphasis on Boeing?ĆÖs long-term commitment to Saudi Arabia, Director of BR&T Global Technology Bill Lyons said.
?Ć£We are honored to be able to partner with King Abdullah University for Science & Technology on this important milestone for our organization.?ĆØBoeing?ĆÖs heightened engagement with KAUST supports the nation?ĆÖs commitment to closing the gap between higher education and the requirements of the job market.
In order to prepare students for the transition from schooling to career, Saudi Arabia aims to have five national universities listed among the top 200 institu-tions worldwide, helping students exceed their potential in global education indicators.
Investment in strategic partnerships with private compa-nies and international firms will be a pillar of educational outreach in Saudi Arabia under Vision 2030.
SAUDI ARABIA FIGHTS EXTREMISM, PROMOTES COEXISTENCE WITH MULTIMEDIA CAMPAIGN
On the heels of Saudi Arabia?ĆÖs participation in the 2016 G20 Summit in China, several global econo-mists praised the Kingdom?ĆÖs conception and imple-mentation of Vision 2030.
?Ć£Vision 2030 is an important commitment for the King-dom to reform in a world where significant supply side reforms have largely stalled.
If the process is a success, then it would be an important example to other emerg-ing markets.?ĆØSAUDI VISION 2030STATUS REPORTJames ReeveDeputy Chief EconomistSamba Financial Group?Ć£As the largest OPEC producer and a regional political heavy weight, Saudi Arabia plays a critical role within the G20.
With its Vision 2030 and its National Trans-formation Program 2020, there is a race against time to implement such a plan.
Hence, the other G19 are monitoring with anticipation how they may participate and benefit from the Saudi vision.
Multiple joint ventures and cooperation memoranda have been signed recently in Japan and China in view of the Saudi Aramco?ĆÖs initial public offering (IPO), the housing boom and  other growing sectors.?ĆØ Basil Al-GhalayiniChief Executive Officer
BMG Financial Group?Ć£Saudi Arabia can play a very impressive role in the G20 proceedings, if it chooses to.
As the largest oil exporter and holding the largest oil reserve, and having become more recently a very important destination for financial capital to purchase gov-ernment bonds, Saudi Arabia can contribute to important discussions about recovering the global economy from its current stagnant position.
Moreover, as G20 countries are encouraged to promote fiscal spending as a way of providing stimulus to the global economy, Saudi Arabia?ĆÖs large reserve holdings can be used for these purposes as well.?ĆØBessma MomaniSenior FellowCentre for International Governance and Innovation ?Ć£Saudi Arabia, through its G20 membership, has played a pivotal role in further boosting the world economy, as the economy of the only Arab coun-try in the group is based on a firm economic and industrial pillar.
The Kingdom?ĆÖs presence in the G20 group, which consists of the world?ĆÖs most powerful economies, provides a constant opportunity to keep in touch with the most powerful political and eco-nomic decision-makers globally, a matter that en-hances its bilateral cooperation with each of them.?ĆØJohn SfakianakisDirector of Economic ResearchGulf Research Center

title: Counterterrorism Update
content:
Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, told the 71st session of the General Assembly of the United Nations on Sept. 21 that defeating terrorism is a priority for Saudi Arabia, which ?Ć£has been among the primary victims of terrorism?ĆØ going back many years.Prince Mohammad said that the Kingdom has been the target of more than 100 terrorist operations since 1992, ?Ć£including 18 carried out by elements with links to ?Ćśa regional country.?ĆÖ?ĆØThe Crown Prince said that the Kingdom has ?Ć£exerted strenuous efforts...to bring about peace and lay the foundations for security and stability since joining the organization, not just in our region, but also all over the world.?ĆØ  The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
Counterterrorism Update
OCTOBER 2016 TOP NEWS
INSIDE THIS ISSUE
Crown Prince Brings Counterterrorism Message to United Nations
Saudi Religious Scholars Defy Threats, Denounce Terrorism ISIL-Related Terror Attacks
Saudi Authorities Arrest 17 in Raids on Daesh Terror Cells  
CIA Director John Brennan Praises Saudi Counterterrorism on ?Ć£Face the Nation?ĆØ 
Saudi Special Forces Secure Pilgrims During Hajj 
Saudi Arabia Fights Extremism, Promotes Coexistence With Multimedia Campaign 
NOTABLE QUOTE
?Ć£Today we are sparing no effort, under the leadership of the Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman Abdulaziz Al-Saud, to secure peace, security and stability ?Ćō not only in our region, but all over the world.
Combatting terrorism is major part of that effort.?ĆØCrown Prince Mohammad bin Naif United Nations General Assembly, September 21, 2016
Crown Prince Brings Counterterrorism Message to United Nations
Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and  Minister of Interior of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, told the 71st session of the General Assembly of the United Nations on Sept.21 that defeating terrorism is a priority for Saudi Arabia, which ?Ć£has been among the primary victims of terrorism?ĆØ  going back many years.
Prince Mohammad said that the Kingdom has been the target of more than 100 terrorist operations since 1992, ?Ć£including 18 carried out by elements with links to  ?Ćśa regional country.?ĆÖ?ĆØThe Crown Prince said that the Kingdom has ?Ć£exerted strenuous efforts?Ć?to bring about peace and lay the foundations for security and stability since joining the organization, not just in our region, but also all over the world.?ĆØ
Saudi Religious Scholars Defy Threats, Denounce Terrorism
Several prominent Saudi religious scholars recently drew the public?ĆÖs attention to the critical importance of the role played by scholars in disseminating factual information and revealing the unethical tactics of terrorists in trying to lure people away from the rightful path of Islam.
Saudi Authorities Arrest 17 in Raids on Daesh Terror Cells
The Ministry of Interior announced on Sept.19 the arrest of 17 suspects in three terror cells who were involved in past terror attacks and were plotting future attacks in Saudi Arabia.
The cells constituted a terror network linked to Daesh (ISIL), and the individuals were arrested in an operation that included a series of raids conducted over several months.
Crown Prince Brings Counterterrorism Message to United Nations
Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, Deputy Prime Minister and Minister of Interior of the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia, told the 71st session of the General Assembly of the United Nations on Sept.21 that defeating terrorism is a priority for Saudi Arabia, which ?Ć£has been among the primary victims of terrorism?ĆØ going back many years.
Prince Mohammad said that the Kingdom has been the target of more than 100 terrorist operations since 1992, ?Ć£including 18 carried out by elements with links to ?Ćśa regional country.?ĆÖ?ĆØ
The Crown Prince said that the Kingdom has ?Ć£exerted strenuous efforts?Ć?to bring about peace and lay the foun-dations for security and stability since joining the organiza-tion, not just in our region, but also all over the world.?ĆØ
?Ć£Saudi security apparatuses have also managed to detect 268 terrorist operations, and thwarted them before they took place,?ĆØ he said.
Prince Mohammad cited the laws, regulations and other measures the Kingdom has enacted to criminalize the sup-port and financing of acts of terrorism and the fact that it has ?Ć£signed on to more than 12 international [counter terrorism] agreements.?ĆØ
Prince Mohammad highlighted the Kingdom?ĆÖs role in the formation of the multi-country Islamic Military Alliance to combat terrorism as well as the agreement Saudi Arabia signed with the .N.to launch the United Nations Count-er-Terrorism Center and the Kingdom?ĆÖs pledge of $110 million to the center to help build the Center?ĆÖs capabilities and effectiveness in helping countries combat terrorism.
Saudi Religious Scholars Defy Threats, Denounce Terrorism
Several prominent Saudi religious scholars recently drew the public?ĆÖs attention to the critical importance of the role played by scholars in disseminating factual information and revealing the unethical tactics of ter-rorists in trying to lure people away from the rightful path of Islam.
Sheikh Abdullah bin Sulaiman Al-Manea, Member of the Council and Adviser to the Royal Court, highlight-ed the role of the authorities at the Ministry of Interior in protecting and ensuring the safety of Saudi Arabia in light of this trend.
?Ć£We are proud of these distin-guished soldiers who bravely protect this country and its people, and they are trusted and appreciated by our leadership.
We are proud to be secure, protected and stable so long as we continue to leave and please God,?ĆØ Sheikh Abdullah said.
Council Member and Royal Court Adviser Sheikh Dr. Al Turki said that the Kingdom was established based on cooperation between princes and scholars, and this approach has continued through various stages of the country?ĆÖs history.
Sheikh Dr. Al Turki stressed the complementary relationship that exists between the Kingdom?ĆÖs leaders and scholars; in the face of increasing terror attacks in the region and throughout the world, scholars actively disseminate the important message about upholding the religion?ĆÖs true principles and staying away from conflict.
Scholars are targeted by terrorists because of their role in revealing the truth behind the terrorists?ĆÖ sinful actions, said Council Member and member of the Standing Committee for Fatwas Sheikh Dr. Saleh bin Fozan Al-Fozan.
He added that scholars play a vital role in promoting awareness among the community about terrorists?ĆÖ tactics. 
Saudi Authorities Arrest 17 in Raids on Daesh Terror Cells
The Ministry of Interior announced on Sept.19 the arrest of 17 suspects in three terror cells who were in-volved in past terror attacks and were plotting future attacks in Saudi Arabia.
The cells constituted a terror network linked to Daesh (ISIL), and the individuals were arrested in an operation that included a series of raids conducted over several months.
Saudi authorities seized pipe bombs, weapons, am-munition, suicide vests and bomb-making materials in the raids.
In a press conference, Ministry of Interi-or spokesman Major General Mansour Al-Turki said the terrorist cells had a list of targets that included civilians, religious scholars and security officers as well as the locations of military installations and economic targets.
He said the suspects were found to have pro-vided Daesh with media and electronic support.
CIA Director John Brennan Praises Saudi Counterterrorism  on ?Ć£Face the Nation?ĆØ
CIA Director John Brennan recently praised Saudi Ara-bia for its close partnership with the United States on counterterrorism.
On the September 11 broadcast of the CBS News program Face the Nation, host John Dick-erson asked: ?Ć£There?ĆÖs a bill that has been through the House and Senate about allowing suing of Saudi Arabia with respect to 9/11.
How does that affect the work you do with Saudi Arabia and intelligence??ĆØ
Brennan responded: ?Ć£Saudi Arabia is one of our  closest partners on counterterrorism.
I have  worked very closely, especially [with] Crown Prince Mohammad bin Naif, over the last 15 years.
And they are truly a good example of how foreign intelligence services can work against these terrorist organiza-tions.
And Saudi Arabia faces a very serious threat from terrorism.?ĆØ
Saudi Special Forces Secure Pilgrims During Hajj
As Saudi Arabia prepared to receive approximately 1.5 million pilgrims from all over the world for the annual Hajj, the Kingdom worked to secure the an-nual religious observance.
Every year, the Kingdom puts a security plan in place that engages a number of highly equipped government agencies in order to maintain safety and security.
One of the most important measures taken this year was the deploy-ment of the Saudi special-forces unit known as the ?Ć£Black Leopards.?ĆØ
The Black Leopards are a highly qualified and well-equipped force who have been trained in rapid- intervention operations.
This special unit was  established in 2012 and was deployed to operate in Hajj this year.
Black Leopards were positioned in Security Control Centers, specifically at the entrances of Holy Sites.
They are trained and equipped to respond to  emergency events and were given the authority to deal directly with any terrorist attacks or other event that may put the pilgrims?ĆÖ safety at risk.
They are supervised by special security agencies and report directly to the Head of General Directorate of Public Security at the Ministry of Interior.
Saudi Arabia Fights Extremism, Promotes Coexistence  with Multimedia Campaign
At a conference earlier this year of the Organization of Islamic Cooperation, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz made a series of remarks about the widespread phenomenon of terrorist recruitment and incitement on the Internet.
He called on Saudi youth to take the initiative to counter extremists?ĆÖ online activity with their own responses by arguing against strident interpreta-tions of Islam and advancing their own, tolerant understanding of the religion in its stead.
He also called on people of good conscience throughout the world ?Ćö including state and non-state elements ?Ćö  to play their part in fighting extremism on the  Internet and social media.
Saudi Arabia has been fighting deviant extremist  ideologies and terrorist recruiting online for many years.
For example, a public service announcement produced and broadcast in Saudi Arabia was recently hailed in the Huffington Post as an example of efforts to curb online extremism.
This public service announcement, released by the King Adulaziz Center for National Dialogue in June 2015, generated more than 150,000 views in the first 48 hours after its release.
The text of the Saudi PSA:What is the greatest destroyer of nations and peoples? Disease? Natural disasters? Famine? Perhaps.
But there is a hidden killer worse than all of them.
A killer many of us don?ĆÖt know, but who has claimed hundreds of thousands of lives ?Ćö children, women, the elderly.
And it doesn?ĆÖt stop at killing people.
It destroys states.
It bleeds their resources, wrecks economies, and stifles knowledge.
It brings states back to zero ?Ćö to less than zero.
Shall I surprise you some more? The killer lives among us.
Right now, at this very moment, he walks around free and loose.
He might be near you, in the same building.
He might be right beside you.
He might be your dearest friend, or your closest relative.
Shall I shock you even more? The hidden killer might be .
YO.
You think you are innocent? Think again.
Go over your convictions and ideas.
Think back on the grudges you bear.
Lots of people like you thought they were innocent, but became wild animals.
They ruined their own states, destroying them with enormity.
Hundreds of thousands of dead and wounded.
This killer, sleeping inside you, wakes up the moment you give up on the value of coexistence.
You transform into a killer, because the fire of hate has been ignited.
Dividing people into categories.
Insularity.
When you fail to live together with people who are different than you, you grow wild.
The result? A devastating war.
Its soldiers are you and thousands like you, all from a single country.
Then come years of conflict.
No one wins except for death.
The country falls behind the rest of the world.
Resources are wasted that could have been invested for everyone to make a better life, to build a solid economy, to strengthen security, to support teaching and knowl-edge.
It might have been possible for everyone together to avoid this disaster if everyone thought about coex-istence and rowed on both sides of the lifeboat, rather than vanish into a whirlpool.
On your way to work, or when you enter your home and are among your family, always ask yourself ?Ćö is a dispute worth losing all of this?Coexistence.
Let?ĆÖs talk to each other, let?ĆÖs live together.

title: Counterterrorism Update
content:
On July 5, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz recommitted the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to ?Ć£strike with an iron fist?ĆØ the terror groups that seek to recruit youths to ?Ć£extremism and violence,?ĆØ and pledged efforts to ?Ć£distance them from masterminds of misleading ideas.?ĆØ  KINGDOM OF SAUDI ARABIA
COUNTERTERRORISM UPDATE
JULY 2016
CONTENTS  SAUDI KING VOWS TO FIGHT EXTREMISTS WITH AN ?Ć£IRON FIST?ĆØSAUDI ARABIA ARRESTS 19 CONSPIRATORS IN TRIPLE BOMBINGSSAUDI GENERAL: KINGDOM IS A TERROR TARGETSaudi Arabia Shares Bank  Records with Department  of JusticeVice-Governor of SAMA  Declares Terror Financing  ?Ć£A Graver Crime?ĆØ Saudi Arabia?ĆÖs Achievements in Combating Terror-FinancingMOI Announces That  Fundraising without Permit is Against the LawInstallation of Surveillance Cameras in All Mosques  Underway
NOTABLE QUOTE  Saudis have become among our ?Ć£ Over the last 15 years, the best counterterrorism partners.?ĆØJohn O. BrennanDirector, Central Intelligence Agency June 11, 2016
TOP NEWS
Saudi King Vows to Fight Extremists with an ?Ć£Iron Fist?ĆØ
On July 5, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz  recommitted  the  Kingdom  of  Saudi  Arabia  to  ?Ć£strike with an iron fist?ĆØ the terror groups that seek to recruit youths to ?Ć£extremism and violence,?ĆØ and pledged efforts to ?Ć£distance them from masterminds of misleading ideas.?ĆØ 
FEATURED NEWS
Saudi Arabia Arrests 19 Conspirators in Triple Bombings
On July 7, the Ministry of Interior announced that investigations have  led  to  the  arrest  of  19  persons  who  were  involved  in  the  recent  terrorist  attacks  in  Madinah,  Qatif  and  Jeddah,  including seven  Saudi  citizens  and  12  Pakistani  nationals.
Those  investigations are ongoing.
Saudi King Vows to Fight  Extremists with an ?Ć£Iron Fist?ĆØ
On July 5, Custodian of the Two Holy Mosques King Salman bin Abdulaziz recommitted the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia to ?Ć£strike with an iron fist?ĆØ the terror groups that seek to recruit youths to ?Ć£extremism and violence,?ĆØ and pledged efforts to ?Ć£distance them from masterminds of misleading ideas.?ĆØ
In a message to the people of Saudi Arabia and all the world?ĆÖs Muslims on the occasion of the Eid Al-Fitr,King Salman urged Muslims to unite and condemned terrorism and extremism, stressing their contradiction to Islam.
?Ć£The sedition witnessed in the Islamic world today requires all of us to exert the utmost effort to unify our voice, close ranks and work together to solve the problems of the Islamic nation, and achieve victory for its issues,?ĆØ  said King Salman.
?Ć£The Kingdom of Saudi Arabia is determined, God  willing, to strike with an iron fist those who target the minds and attitudes of our youth,?ĆØ he said.
?Ć£However, the biggest  challenge facing our Islamic nation is how to keep our youth, the real wealth and hope of the future, away from the dangers facing them ?Ćō particularly extremism 
and violence ?Ćō and distance them from masterminds of misleading ideas that force them to behave in ex-traordinary ways that contradict the principles of our Islamic religion and the pillars and values of our Islamic societies.?ĆØ
King Salman vowed to spare no effort to crush  extremists that use misleading messages to entice impressionable young people to join them.
Saudi General: Kingdom is a Terror Target
During  a  briefing  on  June  8,  the  Ministry  of  Interior?ĆÖs  security spokesman, Major General Mansour Al-Turki said the Kingdom has been a major target of terrorist organizations, but is determined to defeat it and prevent terrorists from using any resources from Saudi Arabia.
?Ć£We  have  faced  more  than  63  terrorist  attacks  by Al-Qaeda  and  ISIL,  26  of  them  just  in  the  last  two years,?ĆØ said Gen.Al-Turki. ?Ć£More than 200 citizens and policemen were killed in terrorist attacks.
More than 2,800 suspects have been arrested since 2015.?ĆØ 
In order to crush terrorism and cut off terror financing, Gen.Al-Turki said the Kingdom has enforced laws to criminalize all terrorist-related activities; including joining, supporting, or recruiting for extremist groups.
LEADER IN THE FIGHT AGAINST DAESH (ISIL)      
?Ć£ The Saudi government took a zero-tolerance stance on ISIL, condemning its activities and participating in coalition military action to defeat the group in Syria and Iraq.
Its external military action against ISIL in Syria as a part of the U.S-led coalition was complemented by an aggressive campaign by both official clerics and King Salman to discredit the group and condemn its activities as acts of terrorism.?ĆØ
U.S Department of State
Country Reports on Terrorism 2015 
June 2, 2016 THE KINGDOM OF SAUDI ARABIA
COUNTERTERRORISM UPDATE
The Kingdom has established a money intelligence 
Investigations are typically done in coordination with the Saudi Monetary  Agency and the Saudi banks.
This has led to convicting more than 226 persons of terrorism financing activities, prosecuting more than 240 suspects, freezing and investigating more than 117  suspected  bank  accounts,  closing  all  unlicensed charity collection locations,?ĆØ said Gen.Al-Turki.
Saudi Arabia Shares Bank  Records with Department  of Justice
As  cited  in  the  U.S  State  Department?ĆÖs  Country  Reports  on  Terrorism  issued  on  June  2,  Saudi  Arabia produced certified bank records in response to a mutual legal assistance request of the United States ?Ć£based on reciprocity and increased cooperation on identification of foreign terrorist fighters traveling to Syria  and  Iraq.?ĆØ  
The  move  is  yet  another  enhance-department to monitor and investigate any suspect ed financial transactions.
-ment in U.S-Saudi cooperation to combat terrorism.
Saudi  Arabia  is  working  with  its  allies  to  deploy new  and  effective  methods  to  prevent  travel  and  support  for  foreign  terrorist  fighters  (FTFs).
Just as  airlines  hold  valuable  data  about  their  passengers, banks can reveal considerable intelligence with regards to the whereabouts, activities and intentions of their account and card holders.
It is hoped that this enhanced  cooperation  between  the  U.S  and  Saudi Arabia will help identify and capture foreign fighters.
According to the International Center for Counterter-rorism (October 2015), it is estimated that there are 30,000 FTFs in Syria and Iraq with Daesh (ISIL).
Vice-Governor of SAMA  Declares Terror Financing  ?Ć£A Graver Crime?ĆØ
The  Eighth  Forum  on  Compliance  and  Combating Money Laundering was held in Riyadh on May 9.
During the event, which focused on best practices in accorddance  with  international  standards,  Vice  Governor Abdulaziz  Saleh  Al  Furaih  noted,  ?Ć£We  cannot  discuss compliance and combating money laundering apart from terrorist financing.
If  money laundering is a crime, terrorist financing is a much graver crime.?ĆØ
The Saudi Arabian Monetary Agency (SAMA) forum 
The latest of these standards to be put into practice by Saudi Arabia include FATF stan-dards on interaction between correspondent banks and money transfer services; FATF customer due dil-igence; and FATF standards on money transfers, particularly in developing controls in order to prevent the risks of illegal transactions.
MOI Announces That Fundraising without Permit is Against the Law
The Ministry of Interior announced on June 11 that all charity fundraising without a permit represents a violation of the Kingdom?ĆÖs laws.
The Ministry stressed that anyone who calls for or collects donations without a license will be arrest-ed and subjected to the laws of the Kingdom.
Furthermore, bank accounts for collecting donations announced on social media and SMS messages will be frozen.
The new regulation follows a decree issued in Feb-on combating money laundering and terror-financ-ruary 2016 that all citizens and NGOs that wish to establish  a  charitable  project  in  another  country must  receive  permission  from  the  Saudi  Ministry of the Interior before setting up operations in that country.
ing  has  gained  prominence  in  recent  years  for  its rigorous conference work to promote the advancement  of  efforts  to  implement  international  compliance standards.
Installation of Surveillance Cameras in All Mosques  Underway
On June 9, Saudi Arabia announced that it would install  surveillance  cameras  inside  and  outside  all mosques  throughout  the  country.
The  long-term plan, announced by the Ministry of Islamic Affairs, includes setting up an operations room with links to 95,000 mosques.
Saudi Arabia?ĆÖs Achievements in Combating Terror-Financing
?Īż Earned observer status in the Financial Action ?Īż  Implemented  Know-Your-Customer  rules  in  Task  Force  (FATF)  in  2015;  aiming  for  full financial and non-financial institutions.
